observe: Everyone gets a slice. Then it's the next one's turn. Nobody holds the Core forever here.
fault: The slice was too big. P1 just ran like it owned the place — that's not round-robin anymore.
repair: Tighten the quantum. Watch everyone actually get their turn.
escape: Keep the slices even — don't let anyone hold too long.

observe: Round-robin scheduling allocates a fixed time quantum to each process in turn.
fault: An oversized quantum caused RR to behave like FCFS.
repair: Run the schedule with a properly sized quantum.
escape: Complete the scheduling run before time expires.

hints: A quantum close to the longest burst time defeats the purpose of round-robin.
